prose:
The Fukui reactivity analysis of nitrobenzene was performed using DFT at the tier 'standard' level of theory (B3LYP functional, def2-TZVP basis set) in the gas phase, with a charge of 0 and multiplicity of 1. The results identify the sites most susceptible to nucleophilic, electrophilic, and radical attack.

The atom with the highest Fukui index for nucleophilic attack (f+) is carbon atom 4 (C4), with a value of 0.49642. This is the most electrophilic site, where a nucleophile is most likely to react.

The atom with the highest Fukui index for electrophilic attack (f-) is carbon atom 5 (C5), with a value of 0.10729. This is the most nucleophilic site, where an electrophile is most likely to react.

The Fukui indices for all atoms are as follows (atom indices are 1-based, matching common chemistry conventions):

| Atom Index | Symbol | f+ (Electrophilic Attack) | f- (Nucleophilic Attack) | f0 (Radical Attack) |
|------------|--------|---------------------------|--------------------------|---------------------|
| 1          | O      | 0.47317                   | 0.09702                  | 0.28509             |
| 2          | O      | 0.47344                   | 0.09677                  | 0.28511             |
| 3          | N      | -0.45670                  | 0.01724                  | -0.21973            |
| 4          | C      | 0.49642                   | -0.01429                 | 0.24106             |
| 5          | C      | 0.11563                   | 0.10729                  | 0.11146             |
| 6          | C      | 0.11588                   | 0.10724                  | 0.11156             |
| 7          | C      | 0.17412                   | 0.09492                  | 0.13452             |
| 8          | C      | 0.17423                   | 0.09489                  | 0.13456             |
| 9          | C      | 0.28914                   | 0.00831                  | 0.14872             |
| 10         | H      | -0.23169                  | 0.07521                  | -0.07824            |
| 11         | H      | -0.23182                  | 0.07520                  | -0.07831            |
| 12         | H      | -0.13372                  | 0.08388                  | -0.02492            |
| 13         | H      | -0.13379                  | 0.08388                  | -0.02496            |
| 14         | H      | -0.12431                  | 0.07243                  | -0.02594            |

No warnings were reported by the tool. The calculation's integrity was verified, confirming charge conservation for the Fukui indices.
